findings.message: Using --chmod with write permissions for group or others is security-sensitive. Non-root users can modify resources, which violates container immutability and allows potential tampering. Use --chmod with restrictive permissions (e.g., 644, 755) or --chown to set ownership without granting write access.

Use --chmod with restrictive permissions (e.g., 644, 755) or --chown to set ownership without granting write access.
